title: diplectria stipularis
side_effects: Diplectria stipularis can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingestion of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact may result in redness, itching, and blistering. Improper use of Diplectria stipularis may also lead to respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause more severe health issues, such as liver and kidney damage.
